summary: Period and frequency are reciprocals: T = 1/f. The period is how long one cycle takes; the frequency is how many cycles fit in a second.
method: Identify whether you are given the period (a time) or the frequency (a rate).
Use SI units: seconds for the period, hertz for the frequency.
Substitute and compute; check that a short period means a high frequency.

example.title: Frequency of a pendulum
steps: The unknown is f, so use f = 1/T.
